meta.title: HSE Skills Roadmap — Re-roofing Block C
meta.subtitle: Recommended skill chain + de-identified run sheet (catalog router)
meta.author: using-hse-skills (catalog router)
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label ([SITE-1], [ROLE: site manager], [CONTRACTOR]). The captured handoff facts were pseudonymized before this roadmap was written; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: Routing for: re-roofing fall-from-height at [SITE-1], own workers + [CONTRACTOR], UK, construction. Goal: make the job safe end to end — risk assessment, safe-work method, permit, and a toolbox talk the crew can sign. This router produces no HSE artifact itself; it recommends the ordered chain below and hands the de-identified context over.
SEQUENCE MAP:  risk-assessment → job-safety-analysis → permit-to-work → toolbox-talk

rows: Establishes the hazard/control baseline for fall-from-height before any method or permit.
Breaks the agreed control set into a step-by-step safe-work method for the crew.
Authorises the work-at-height task with the Step 1–2 controls as permit conditions.
Briefs and signs off the night crew on the specific hazards and controls before they start.
sections.caption: Ordered recommendation with per-skill rationale (ROUTE-03)

items: WHY: Establishes the hazard/control baseline for the re-roofing task at height.
RUN: /risk-assessment (or: use the risk-assessment skill).

THEN PASTE: shared context — strip-and-re-roof Block C at [SITE-1], fall-from-height, own workers + [CONTRACTOR], UK, construction; lead = [ROLE: site manager] — plus full task steps and the 5×5 matrix.
CARRY-IN: none — first step.
DEPENDENCY: Independent.
FEEDS →: Step 2 (the agreed control set + residual risks).
WHY: Breaks the control set into a step-by-step safe-work method for the crew.
RUN: /job-safety-analysis.
THEN PASTE: same shared context (re-roofing Block C at [SITE-1], own workers + [CONTRACTOR], UK, construction; lead = [ROLE: site manager]) plus the agreed control set from Step 1.
DEPENDENCY: Dependent — run after Step 1.
FEEDS →: Step 3 (the safe-work method steps + control owners).

sections.text: CARRY-IN — Step 2: ⚠ attach the risk-assessment OUTPUT from Step 1 — the control set + residual risks.

items: WHY: Authorises the work-at-height task with the Step 1–2 controls as conditions.
RUN: /permit-to-work.
THEN PASTE: same shared context plus the working-at-height permit type and validity window.
DEPENDENCY: Dependent — run after Steps 1 and 2.
FEEDS →: Step 4 (the permitted controls + sign-on conditions).

sections.text: CARRY-IN — Step 3: ⚠ attach the job-safety-analysis OUTPUT from Step 2 — the method steps + control owners — and the risk-assessment residual risks from Step 1.

items: WHY: Briefs and signs off the night crew on the specific hazards and controls.
RUN: /toolbox-talk.
THEN PASTE: same shared context plus tonight's crew and the top three controls to brief.
DEPENDENCY: Dependent — run after Step 3.
FEEDS →: none — final step; the crew signs and starts work.

sections.text: CARRY-IN — Step 4: ⚠ attach the permit-to-work OUTPUT from Step 3 — the permitted controls + sign-on conditions the crew must acknowledge.

items: On a host with the Skill tool: say "go" and the router launches Step 1's skill seeded with that block; after each output returns, it offers the next step.
Off-platform / fresh chat: copy a step block into a new chat and run the named skill; for Steps 2–4, attach the prior step's output as the CARRY-IN instructs.
Each target skill's own intake echoes these facts back and asks only the deep, per-artifact details this router deferred (scoring, baseline, evidence, obligations, competency, cadence).
